vulnerabilities.title: Arbitrary Code Execution
vulnerabilities.description: PyYAML 5.3.1 and earlier allows remote code execution via yaml.load() without a Loader parameter. An attacker can craft a malicious YAML payload.

vulnerabilities.title: Buffer Overflow
vulnerabilities.description: Pillow before 8.1.1 allows a regular expression denial of service (ReDoS) attack via a crafted image file because the regex in PIL/SgiImagePlugin.py does not properly handle large SGI data.

vulnerabilities.title: NULL Pointer Dereference
vulnerabilities.description: In the cryptography package before 3.3.2 for Python, certain sequences of update calls to symmetrically encrypt multi-GB values could result in an integer overflow and buffer overflow, as demonstrated by the Fernet class.

vulnerabilities.title: Unintended Proxy Settings Leak
vulnerabilities.description: Requests package prior to 2.31.0 forwards Proxy-Authorization headers to destination servers when following redirects. This may expose proxy credentials to third-party servers.

vulnerabilities.title: XPath Injection
vulnerabilities.description: An XSS vulnerability was discovered in python-lxml's clean module versions before 4.6.3. When disabling the safe_attrs_only and forms arguments, the clean_html function does not properly sanitize href attributes, allowing XSS in HTML5 contexts.

vulnerabilities.title: Improper Input Validation
vulnerabilities.description: Flask 2.0.1 does not properly handle certain redirect URLs which could result in open redirect in specific configurations.

vulnerabilities.title: Sandbox Bypass Leading to Arbitrary Code Execution
vulnerabilities.description: Jinja2 before 3.1.4 does not properly escape the name attribute of html elements in the xmlattr filter, allowing attackers to inject arbitrary HTML attributes which may enable XSS.

vulnerabilities.title: Remote Code Execution
vulnerabilities.description: Applications using Werkzeug with multipart form parsing enabled are vulnerable to resource exhaustion when processing specially crafted multipart data.

vulnerabilities.title: SQL Injection via ORM
vulnerabilities.description: SQLAlchemy 1.3.x does not warn when using text() constructs with user-supplied data. Code using text() without bound parameters may be vulnerable to SQL injection.

false_positives_candidates.finding: Flask debug mode enabled detected in binary scan
false_positives_candidates.reason_flagged: Static analysis of the binary found references to debug mode symbols
false_positives_candidates.context: The scanner detected the string 'debug' in the Flask package binary. This is part of Flask's own internal debug-mode documentation strings and does not indicate an actual misconfiguration in user code.
false_positives_candidates.analyst_note: CANDIDATE: Is this a genuine vulnerability? Justify your decision.

false_positives_candidates.finding: Insecure use of SSL/TLS — verify=False detected
false_positives_candidates.reason_flagged: Pattern match found 'verify=False' in requests source code
false_positives_candidates.context: The scanner matched 'verify=False' inside the requests library's own test suite (tests/test_utils.py), not in application code.
false_positives_candidates.analyst_note: CANDIDATE: Is this a genuine vulnerability? Justify your decision.

false_positives_candidates.finding: Use of yaml.load() without Loader argument
false_positives_candidates.reason_flagged: Pattern match found yaml.load() call without explicit Loader in app code
false_positives_candidates.context: The scanner found a yaml.load() call in a commented-out code block that was left in the file but is never executed. The active code path uses yaml.safe_load().
false_positives_candidates.analyst_note: CANDIDATE: How do you classify this finding? Is it exploitable? What action would you take?
